message:
fix(http2): handle RST_STREAM during body reads and harden SETTINGS validation (bd-2c9t)

Two HTTP/2 conformance improvements in the H2 server loop:

1. RST_STREAM during body reads: When a client cancels a request
   mid-stream (sends RST_STREAM while the server is still reading
   DATA frames), the server now correctly breaks out of the body
   read loop and skips to the next stream instead of returning an
   error. This applies to all three server code paths (app, closure,
   and TLS).

2. SETTINGS frame validation: Extract duplicated inline SETTINGS
   checks into validate_settings_frame() helper that:
   - Validates stream_id == 0 (protocol requirement per RFC 9113)
   - Rejects ACK frames with non-empty payloads (RFC 9113 §6.5:
     "Receipt of a SETTINGS frame with the ACK flag set and a
     length field value other than 0 MUST be treated as a
     connection error")
   - Returns a bool indicating ACK status
   Replaces 6 copies of the same inline validation logic across
   the app, closure, and TLS server paths.

- Add assert_connection_closed() test helper for protocol violation
  assertions
- Add unit tests for validate_settings_frame edge cases (valid
  non-ack, valid empty ack, non-zero stream rejection, non-empty
  ack payload rejection)
- Add integration tests verifying the server correctly closes the
  connection when receiving SETTINGS ACK with payload during body
  reads

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
